items.title: Villager injured while driving away elephant herd in Sungai Siput
items.summary: The Wildlife and National Parks Department (Perhilitan) said the incident occurred at about 9pm when a herd of four elephants entered the area behind villagers' homes and began feeding on cassava and banana crops. The statement said the victim suffered injuries, bleeding and lost consciousness during the attack. Perhilitan said drone monitoring showed that the elephant herd had since left the village area.

items.title: Motorcyclist found dead hours after crash in Alor Gajah
items.summary: ALOR GAJAH: A motorcyclist was found dead by the roadside about two hours after he is believed to have crashed along Jalan Air Tanai in Air Paabas here early today. District police chief Superintendent Ahmad Abu Bakar said preliminary investigations found that the victim was riding a Modenas Kriss motorcycle from Kampung Air Tanai towards Jalan Air Paabas. He said the victim, who was single and lived in Kampung Air Tanai, is believed to have left home to meet a friend before the accident.

items.title: Ukrainians protest for second day, call to replace army chief
items.summary: KYIV: Protesters in Kyiv called for the dismissal of Ukraine's commander-in-chief, Oleksandr Syrskyi, after President Volodymyr Zelenskiy's removal of a popular defence minister sparked outrage and demands for a more modern approach to warfare. "Syrskyi had conflicts not only with the defence minister, but also with brigade and corps commanders. Protesters rallied for a second day outside Zelenskiy's office, chanting "Syrskyi away" and "Bring Fedorov back."

items.title: Kindergarten linked to neglect allegations told to halt operations pending probe
items.summary: KUCHING: The Welfare Department has advised a Langkawi kindergarten implicated in allegations of child neglect and inappropriate treatment to halt operations temporarily until police complete their investigations. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said that while the kindergarten is regulated by the Education Ministry, Welfare Deparment remains involved in child protection matters and is collaborating with relevant authorities in the investigation. She said the Welfare Department, through its child protection officers, was collaborating with the police and the District Education Office to collect relevant information and prepare the reports required for the ongoing investigation.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Full list of candidates for 36 seats
items.summary: SEREMBAN: The Negri Sembilan election began today with the nomination process taking place simultaneously at eight nomination centres across the state. The Election Commission (EC) confirmed that 103 candidates from various political parties and independent candidates are eligible to contest. The following are the candidates contesting each state assembly seat: N01 CHENNAH Siow Kong Choon - BN Loke Siew Fook - PH N02 PERTANG Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias - BN Mohd Umry Abdul Khois - PH Mohd Faizal Fadli Mohd Idrus - Bersatu N03 SUNGAI LUI Datuk Mohd Razi Mohd Ali - BN Zainal Fikri Abd Kadir - PH Mazrulhisham Abd Mansor - Bersatu N04 KLAWANG Datuk Bakri Sawir - PH Danni Rais - PN Muhammad Adib Musa - Bersatu N05 SERTING Fairuz Isa - PN Yaacob Mahmood - PH Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh - Bersatu N06 PALONG Datuk Mustapha Nagoor - BN Zahin Zinal Abidin - PH Rebin Birham - Bersatu N07 JERAM PADANG Datuk Zaidy Abdul Kadir - BN G. Manivannan - PH R. Sri Sanjeevan - Bersatu Dayana Dal - Parti Asli N08 BAHAU Teo Kok Seong - PH Chong Fui Ming - BN N09 LENGGENG Datuk Mohd Asna Amin - BN Zarinna Abu Zarin - PH Zool Amali Hussin - Bersatu N10 NILAI Arul Kumar Jambunathan - PH Lai Chien Kong - BN Datuk V Saravanan Kumar - Bersatu Zamani Ibrahim - Parti Barisan Jemaah Islamiah Se-Malaysia (BERJASA) Omar Mohd Isa - Independent N11 LOBAK Chew Seh Yong - PH Dr P Kumar - PN N12 TEMIANG Ho Weng Wah - PH Leaw Kok Chan - BN Fazly Hamid - Bersatu N13 SIKAMAT Datuk Tun Faisal Ismail Aziz - Bersatu Nor Azman Mohamad - PH Datuk Razali Abu Samah - PN N14 AMPANGAN Datuk Dr Rafie Abd Malek - PN Datuk Muhammad Nazri Kassim - PH Noor'azah Harun - Bersatu N15 JUASSEH Mohd Aidil Abdullah - PH Datuk Ismail Lasim - BN Mohd Zuhaimi Md Yusof - Bersatu N16 SRI MENANTI Mohd Kamarul Arifin Mohd Wafa - PH Muhammad Sufian Maradzi - BN Datuk Seri Megat D. Shahriman Zaharidin - Bersatu N17 SENALING Mohd Hanis Mohd Alimin - PH Mohamad Qayyum Abd Jalil - BN Mohd Izaffi Istear Khan - Bersatu N18 PILAH Datuk Noorzunita Begum Mohd Ibrahim - PH Lisa Mat Yassin - BN N19 JOHOL Mohd Zailan Mohd Munawar - PH Datuk Saiful Yazan Sulaiman - BN Datuk Ahmad Faez Abdul Razak - PH Mohamad Hanifah Abu Baker - Bersatu Siti Umaira Hasim - BN N21 BUKIT KEPAYANG Nicole Tan Lee Koon - PH Lee Boon Shian - PN N22 RAHANG Tang Jay Son - Bersatu S Tinagaran - Parti Sosialis Malaysia (PSM) N23 MAMBAU Lee Kai Yet - PH N Sarawanan - Bersatu Erick Michael - PN N24 SEREMBAN JAYA Datuk R Thinalan - BN R Mahendran - Bersatu S Mugunthan - PH N25 PAROI Kamarul Ridzuan Mohd Zin - PN Ahmad Shahir Mohd Shah - PH Mohd Nazree Mohd Yunus - Bersatu N26 CHEMBONG Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris - BN Danish Nazran Murad - PH N27 RANTAU Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan - BN Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi - PH N28 KOTA Suhaimi Aini - BN Muhammad Allif Ibrahim - PH Akmal Noradzmi Abd Rahim - Bersatu N29 CHUAH Datuk Yew Boon Lye (PH) Pau Jeou Ching - BN N30 LUKUT Choo Ken Hwa - PH Sathes Kumar Nilameham - PN Teo Seng Li - Independent N31 BAGAN PINANG Abd Fatah Zakaria - PN Nasir Rahman - PH Sheikh Junaidy Jamaludin - Bersatu N32 LINGGI Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli - BN Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun - PH Datuk Zamri Md Said - Bersatu N33 SRI TANJUNG Datuk Dr G Rajasekaran - PH A. Achutan - BN M. Leevineshwaraan - Bersatu Datuk A Saravanan - Independent Islah Wahyudi Zainudin - Independent N34 GEMAS Ridzuan Ahmad - PN Siti Aishah Seman @ Othman - PH Azman Abdullah - Bersatu N35 GEMENCHEH Suhaimizan Bizar - BN Datuk Abd Latif A. Tambi - PH N36 REPAH Veerapan Superamaniam - PH Koh Kim Swee - BN © New Straits Times Press (M) Bhd

items.title: Too much, not too little: Deputy premier Dr Sim says overeating is now Sarawak’s bigger health challenge
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — Public health policies should place greater emphasis on tackling overnutrition, as it is a major contributor to the rising incidence of diabetes among Sarawakians, said Deputy Premier Datuk Amar Dr Sim Kui Hian. Dr Sim said while undernutrition remains an issue, it affects only a small percentage of the population compared with overnutrition, which now requires greater attention. Dr Sim said the prevalence of diabetes in Sarawak is between 17 and 18 per cent, compared with about 21 per cent nationwide.

items.title: Roofs torn off as fierce winds lash Sibu Jaya, no injuries reported
items.summary: SIBU, July 18 —Several houses in Sibu Jaya sustained roof damage after strong winds accompanied by a heavy downpour swept through the area at about 3pm today. Most of the affected homeowners were away when the storm struck, leaving neighbours to witness roofs being ripped off by the powerful winds. A resident, who declined to be named, said the weather deteriorated rapidly before strong winds battered the neighbourhood.

items.title: Muhyiddin claims Bersatu is Parliament’s only true Opposition amid shifting alliances
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has claimed Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia is now the country’s only “standalone” Opposition party, arguing that it must remain independent as the country’s sole credible check on the government amid shifting political alliances. “In fact, the challenges are even greater now because Bersatu is the only standalone Opposition party in the Malaysian Parliament,” he wrote. Muhyiddin argued that Pakatan Harapan (PH) and Barisan Nasional (BN) were both part of the federal government, while Perikatan Nasional’s (PN) political cooperation with BN had, in his view, effectively turned the coalition into a supporter of the government.

items.title: Three Form One boys in Kangar under police investigation over alleged bullying that left classmate with fractured ankle
items.summary: KANGAR, July 18 — Three Form One students at a secondary school here have been remanded for two days from today to assist investigations into the alleged bullying of a schoolmate that left him with a fractured left ankle. Kangar police chief ACP Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police received a report on the incident at 11.27 pm on Thursday. He said the victim’s 39-year-old mother had earlier been informed by his class teacher at 12.28 pm that her son, also a Form One student, had slipped while entering the classroom and was unable to walk.

items.title: Dropped as Johor exco, Aznan backs successor
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: Former Johor education and information committee chairman Aznan Tamin has expressed confidence in Dr Muhammad Naqib Md Ghazali, who replaced him in the state exco line-up. Aznan said the trust placed in the new exco was a great responsibility, as its members would have to ensure Johor continued to progress, bring prosperity to the people and realise the Maju Johor 2030 agenda. During his time in office from 2024 to 2026, Aznan spearheaded several high-impact initiatives, including Sekolah Rintis Bangsa Johor, the Johor Talent Development Council and Insentif Mahasiswa Anak Johor.

items.title: Busker’s bid for Sikamat ends before it begins after election paperwork snag
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Government retiree Bujang Abu’s aspiration to contest as an independent candidate for the Sikamat state seat in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election fell through after documents required by the Election Commission (EC) were found to be incomplete. “During the nomination process, I was informed that I had not submitted the expenditure statement for the 2023 state election. He previously contested the Sikamat state seat in the 2013 General Election (GE) and the 2023 State Election but was unsuccessful on both occasions.

items.title: Form One student suffers fractured ankle in bullying case, three detained
items.summary: KANGAR: Three Form One students have been detained to assist investigations into the alleged bullying of a schoolmate that left him with a fractured left ankle. District police chief assistant commissioner Yusharifuddin Mohd Yusop said police received a report on the incident at 11.27pm on Thursday. He said the victim's 39-year-old mother had earlier been informed by his class teacher at 12.28pm that her son, also a Form One student, had slipped while entering the classroom and was unable to walk.

items.title: Muhyiddin says Bersatu remains sole opposition voice
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Bersatu president Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin has positioned his party as the country's only opposition force, claiming Perikatan Nasional had indirectly become pro-government through its political cooperation with Barisan Nasional. His remarks came after Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang said PN would assist BN during the Negri Sembilan election campaign. Muhyiddin said Pakatan Harapan and BN were part of the government at the federal and state levels, while PN could no longer claim complete independence after working with BN.

items.title: New Seremban orthopaedic centre launching this year aims to cut treatment costs by up to 50 per cent
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Bank Rakyat has provided financing totalling RM1.42 million to Limbcraft Innovation Sdn Bhd to develop an affordable orthopaedic centre here that is expected to offer treatment and surgery costs up to 50 per cent lower, depending on the type of procedure. Entrepreneur and Cooperative Development Minister Steven Sim said the financing was channelled for the renovation of the premises as well as the purchase of machinery and equipment for Axis Orthopaedic Specialist Centre & Ambulatory Care Centre, which is expected to be launched this year. He said the centre would be equipped with an operation theatre, day treatment wards and robotic rehabilitation facilities, while the financing was provided at a low profit rate of four per cent per annum.

items.title: MyCSP: Courier safety training programme opens for registration
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Postal Forum, a self-regulatory body designated by the Malaysian Communications and Multimedia Commission (MCMC), today soft-launched registration for the Malaysia Courier Safety Passport (MyCSP), a structured safety training programme developed specifically for the courier industry. In a statement today, Postal Forum said the MyCSP comprises six core modules, namely, Occupational Safety and Health; hazard and risk management in courier operations, basic ergonomics, road safety awareness, high-risk driving behaviours, and risk management and prevention. "Together, these modules provide participants with practical knowledge to improve workplace and road safety while supporting compliance with industry safety standards.

items.title: MACC steps in over KWAP’s RM200m investment loss linked to Indonesia’s eFishery
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into the RM200 million investment loss suffered by the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP) involving Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abd Halim Aman said an investigation team from the commission’s headquarters was formed yesterday to conduct a comprehensive review and assessment of the matter. The ministry said a consortium of investors, including KWAP, had taken firm action, including pursuing legal proceedings, efforts to recover funds, conducting internal governance reviews and strengthening controls to protect investment interests.

items.title: New Johor exco line-up to deliver manifesto pledges, drive Maju Johor 2030 agenda
items.summary: JOHOR BARU: The newly appointed Johor state executive council (exco) members are determined to fulfil all pledges made in the manifesto for the recent state election and advance the Maju Johor 2030 agenda, ensuring that development benefits continue to reach Bangsa Johor. "In the next four years, we must ensure that Johor's (development) progresses at a faster pace. He said focus would also be given to continuing the Rumah Kasih Johor and Rumah Transit Bangsa Johor initiatives, as well as fulfilling all manifesto pledges made during the recent Johor state election.

items.title: Port, industry and jobs: Aminuddin unveils Linggi plans if he wins state poll
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 18 — Negeri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun has outlined plans to develop a port and an industrial area in the Linggi state constituency if he is given the mandate in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. The Negeri Sembilan Pakatan Harapan (PH) chairman said the proposed projects formed part of a broader development plan aimed at improving infrastructure and creating greater economic opportunities for residents in the constituency. Aminuddin acknowledged that contesting in what is widely regarded as a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold against incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli would not be easy, but expressed confidence that the PH election machinery would ensure a smooth campaign.

items.title: Johor's new exco members vow to accelerate economic transformation over next four years
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — The newly appointed Johor State Executive Council (Exco) members are determined to fulfil all pledges made in the manifesto for the recent state election and advance the Maju Johor 2030 agenda, ensuring that development benefits continue to reach Bangsa Johor. “In the next four years, we must ensure that Johor’s (development) progresses at a faster pace. He said focus would also be given to continuing the Rumah Kasih Johor and Rumah Transit Bangsa Johor initiatives, as well as fulfilling all manifesto pledges made during the recent Johor state election.

items.title: Malaysian ICC staffer breaks silence over sexual misconduct allegations against chief prosecutor Karim Khan
items.summary: Sarah’s appearance was her first public account of allegations that have dominated the ICC for months and triggered an unprecedented disciplinary process against its chief prosecutor. The Guardian first reported the allegations last year. Sarah, 39, served as Khan’s direct special assistant between 2023 and 2024 after several years working at the ICC.

items.title: Trump threatens to increase Canada tariffs over wildfire smoke pollution
items.summary: NEW YORK: US President Donald Trump threatened on Friday a tariff increase on Canada to cover the cost of smoke pollution from wildfires that have choked large parts of the United States. Dense wildfire smoke billowing down from Canada and northern Minnesota has set off unhealthy air quality alerts across the US. Canada's emergency management minister Eleanor Olszewski said Canada and the United States were in "constant contact," pointing toward their "long history of working together to fight wildfires."

items.title: N9 polls: PH candidates in Jempol pledge to address infrastructure, Felda welfare issues
items.summary: JEMPOL: Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidates contesting the Negri Sembilan state election in the four state seats under the Jempol parliamentary constituency have pledged to focus on resolving structural issues, improving basic amenities and safeguarding the welfare of Felda settlers. For PH's Jeram Padang state seat candidate, G. Manivannan, his main focus is on addressing issues related to employment opportunities, education and infrastructure, which he described as the community's basic needs. Meanwhile, PH's Serting state seat candidate, Yaacob Mahmood, who has lived in Bandar Baru Serting for 43 years, is committed to championing the welfare of Felda settlers, particularly the second generation.

items.title: [UPDATED] RM200mil KWAP losses: MACC opens probe into eFishery investment
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Malaysian Anti-Corruption Commission (MACC) has launched an investigation into the reported RM200 million investment losses suffered by the Retirement Fund (Incorporated) (KWAP), involving Indonesian aquaculture technology company eFishery. MACC chief commissioner Datuk Seri Abdul Halim Aman said an investigation team had been formed to conduct a comprehensive review and examination of the matter. The ministry said the Indonesian aquaculture startup's management had manipulated its financial statements.

items.title: Royal visit lifts spirits of three-year-old Sungai Petani Proton X50 crash survivor and her family
items.summary: Her aunt, Siti Nor Atikah Ahmad Syukri, 32, said Aulia Sofea Ahmad Shafiq was discharged from Sultanah Bahiyah Hospital in Alor Setar on July 2 after nearly a month of treatment and has since become increasingly active. However, she said Aulia Sofea is still unable to walk because both her legs were fractured in the crash. Earlier, the Raja Muda of Kedah, Tengku Sarafudin Badlishah ibni Al Aminul Karim Sultan Sallehuddin, Raja Puan Muda of Kedah, Che Puan Muda Zaheeda Mohamad Ariff, and their daughter, Tunku Zara Bahiyah, visited Aulia Sofea.

items.title: MCA Youth sec-gen told to stay away from Negri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: MCA Youth secretary-general Saw Yee Fung said she had been told she could excuse herself from Barisan Nasional's (BN) campaign for the Negri Sembilan election after questioning the coalition's cooperation with Pas. Saw had questioned whether the party leadership endorsed BN's cooperation with Pas ahead of the Negri Sembilan election. She also said no party leader had responded to her concerns on the night BN announced its candidates for the Negri Sembilan election.

items.title: Founding father: Statues of Myanmar's Aung San disappear
items.summary: YANGON, Myanmar: Statues of independence hero Aung San are quietly disappearing across Myanmar in a contest over his legacy as father of both the nation and of detained democracy figurehead Aung San Suu Kyi. The government has acknowledged taking down some statues it said had "incorrect proportions and shapes." - Historical legacy - While in power from 2016-2020, during Myanmar's decade-long experiment with democracy, Suu Kyi's National League for Democracy (NLD) put up statues and also returned Aung San's face to local currency notes.

items.title: N9 polls: Youngest candidate in five-cornered Sri Tanjung fight
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The 16th Negri Sembilan state election has spotlighted M. Leevineshwaraan, the youngest candidate contesting for a seat and seeking the people's mandate. In the 15th state polls in 2023, Muhammad Syakir Fitri Sadri held the record as the youngest candidate, contesting the Paroi state seat as an Independent at the age of 25. The Rantau state seat, within the Rembau parliamentary constituency, has 34,831 eligible voters, while Gemencheh, within the Tampin parliamentary constituency, has 24,916 eligible voters.

items.title: PAS eyes wider Sarawak presence with plan to contest eight seats in next state election
items.summary: MIRI, July 18 — PAS is aiming to contest at least eight seats in the next Sarawak state election following a review of its performance in previous polls Its Sarawak commissioner, Mohammad Arifiriazul Paijo, said the party began preparing its election machinery early this year in anticipation of the state election, which he expects to be held by the end of the year. “In the 2016 state election, PAS contested 11 seats, while in the 2021 election we contested only one. “After conducting a post-mortem and analysing our previous election performance, Sarawak PAS has now set a target of contesting at least eight seats in the next state election,” he told reporters after the PAS Youth Wing Convention at a hotel here on Saturday (July 18).

items.title: Motorcyclist remains in ICU after fatal East Coast Highway RXZ convoy crash
items.summary: Kuantan police chief Assistant Commissioner Ashari Abu Samah said five victims remained in hospital, with three being treated at Tengku Ampuan Afzan Hospital (HTAA), including one in the ICU. Another injured victim is receiving treatment at a private hospital in Kuantan, while one is recuperating at Sultanah Nur Zahirah Hospital in Kuala Terengganu. "So far, only five remain in hospital and eight injured riders have been discharged from hospital," he told the New Straits Times.

items.title: ‘That’s not true’: Hadi rejects Muhyiddin’s ‘toxic’ label for Perikatan under PAS
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — PAS president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has rejected Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin’s claim that Perikatan Nasional (PN) has become “toxic” under the Islamist party’s leadership, blaming Bersatu instead for the deepening rift within the Opposition coalition. Speaking after accompanying PN candidates during the nomination process for the Negeri Sembilan state election today, Hadi dismissed the allegation outright. Muhyiddin said Bersatu would explore forming a new political coalition with several parties after the state election, arguing the existing partnership was no longer functioning as intended.

items.title: BN to launch Negri Sembilan election manifesto on July 24
items.summary: JELEBU: Negri Sembilan Barisan Nasional (BN) will launch its manifesto for the state election on July 24. Negri Sembilan Umno liaison committee chairman Datuk Seri Jalaluddin Alias said the manifesto would focus on various aspects, including the state development agenda, education, the people's well-being and administrative stability. He said the manifesto would serve as a continuation of the approach implemented during the previous state administration, while also introducing several improvements based on the current needs of the people.

items.title: BN candidate says Linggi stronghold cannot be taken for granted
items.summary: PORT DICKSON: Despite Linggi being regarded as a Barisan Nasional (BN) stronghold, incumbent Datuk Mohd Faizal Ramli is not taking the challenge lightly in the Negri Sembilan election. This is especially so with caretaker Negri Sembilan Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun entering the race for Pakatan Harapan (PH), alongside his former contender in the 2023 election, Datuk Zamri Md Said, who is contesting again. Faizal said he had been entrusted by the party to defend the Linggi seat and would work closely with the entire BN machinery to ensure the party retained the constituency.

items.title: BN-PN understanding in Negri polls not a merger, says Wee
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The strategic understanding between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional for the Negri Sembilan election does not amount to a merger, says MCA president Datuk Seri Dr Wee Ka Siong. He said the arrangement was aimed at avoiding multi-cornered contests that could split votes and lead to political instability, while allowing the parties to work towards forming a stable and capable government in Negri Sembilan. Wee said MCA's participation in the Aug 1 polls was to ensure that the interests of the Chinese community, Malaysia's multiracial society, vernacular education and religious freedom continued to have representation and a voice in government.

items.title: BN will run a civil campaign in Negeri Sembilan, says Umno veep
items.summary: TAMPIN, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) will ensure that the Negeri Sembilan state election campaign is conducted in a healthy and prudent manner throughout the 14-day campaign period, said Umno vice-president Datuk Seri Johari Abdul Ghani. He said all BN machinery must prioritise democratic practices by approaching voters in a mature and civilised manner to ensure that the people can make the best choice. “We have two BN candidates, one in Gemencheh and one in Repah.

items.title: Police hail smooth Negeri Sembilan nominations, urge candidates to avoid 3R issues
items.summary: PORT DICKSON, July 18 — The nomination of candidates for the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election at eight nomination centres today went smoothly, with no untoward incidents reported, police said. State police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said supporters of all contesting parties complied with police instructions, and the situation remained under control throughout the nomination process from morning until noon. “Each nomination centre recorded between 1,000 and 2,500 supporters.

items.title: Minister: Langkawi kindergarten told to suspend operations during child abuse investigation
items.summary: KUCHING, July 18 — The Social Welfare Department (JKM) has advised a Langkawi kindergarten implicated in allegations of child neglect and inappropriate treatment to halt operations temporarily until police complete their investigations. Women, Family and Community Development Minister Datuk Seri Nancy Shukri said that while the kindergarten is regulated by the Education Ministry, JKM remains involved in child protection matters and is collaborating with relevant authorities in the investigation. She said JKM, through its child protection officers, was collaborating with the police and the District Education Office to collect relevant information and prepare the reports required for the ongoing investigation.

items.title: Hadi dismisses Muhyiddin's 'toxic PN' claim, points finger at Bersatu
items.summary: JEMPOL: Pas president Tan Sri Abdul Hadi Awang has rejected Tan Sri Muhyiddin Yassin's claim that Perikatan Nasional (PN), under the Islamist party's leadership, has become "toxic". "What happened was Bersatu's misconduct in the cooperation," he told reporters after accompanying PN candidates during the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election here today. (hyperlink: https://www.nst.com.my/news/nation/2026/07/1491090/muhyiddin-pn-toxic-under-pas-leadership-bersatu-eyes-new-coalition) Muhyiddin also accused Pas of leaving Bersatu in the lurch after failing to continue seat negotiations ahead of the Negri Sembilan election.

items.title: Johor Regent tells new exco to put people's interests first
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: The Regent of Johor, Tunku Ismail Sultan Ibrahim, has urged the new Johor executive council (exco) to ensure every policy and decision benefits the state and is guided by the interests of the people. In his decree today, he said exco members must go to the ground to identify and understand the needs of the community. "His Royal Highness Tunku Mahkota Ismail, the Acting Sultan of Johor, has reminded all members of the Johor exco to always discharge their entrusted duties and responsibilities with the utmost dedication, integrity and sincerity.

items.title: Johor Regent reminds new exco members to keep feet on the ground and focus on public’s needs
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — The Regent of Johor, Tunku Mahkota Ismail, has reminded all state executive council (Exco) members to carry out their duties with dedication, integrity and sincerity, while always prioritising the people’s well-being and the state’s progress. In a post on his official Facebook page, His Royal Highness said every policy and decision made must be rooted in the people’s interests and benefit Johor as a whole. The royal reminder came during the Exco appointment and oath-taking ceremony at Balai Mengadap, Istana Bukit Serene earlier today, where 10 Johor assemblymen took their oaths of office before His Highness.

items.title: The rookies and the veterans: Negeri Sembilan polls’ age game gets interesting
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — The 16th Negeri Sembilan state election has put the spotlight on M. Leevineshwaraan, the youngest candidate contesting for a seat and seeking the people’s mandate. In the 15th state polls in 2023, Muhammad Syakir Fitri Sadri held the record as the youngest candidate, contesting the Paroi state seat as an Independent at the age of 25. The Rantau state seat, within the Rembau parliamentary constituency, has 34,831 eligible voters, while Gemencheh, within the Tampin parliamentary constituency, has 24,916 eligible voters.

items.title: [UPDATED] Police rescue kidnap-for-ransom victim, detain six suspects
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR: Police rescued a kidnapped man within 48 hours and arrested six suspects following a RM7 million ransom demand. Federal police Criminal Investigation Department director Datuk M. Kumar said the victim was rescued from a residential unit in i-City, Shah Alam, at 1am today. He said a team comprising Selangor Criminal Investigation Department and Special Branch personnel later raided the residential unit and rescued the victim.

items.title: Negeri Sembilan polls in numbers: 103 candidates confirmed, 21 seats head into three-way fights
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — The Election Commission (EC) has confirmed that 103 candidates are eligible to contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, with 21 of the 36 state seats set for three-cornered fights. He revealed that Pakatan Harapan (PH) is dominating the race with 36 candidates, followed by Barisan Nasional (BN) with 25, while Bersatu and Perikatan Nasional (PN) have fielded 24 and 11 candidates respectively. Ramlan revealed that 21 state seats will see three-cornered fights, while 11 seats will be straight one-on-one contests.

items.title: When silence enables bullying
items.summary: BULLYING has evolved. Bullying is about power. The most painful form of bullying is often emotional and relational.

items.title: Four new faces, six familiar names: Onn Hafiz unveils Johor exco members for his second term
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — The Johor State Executive Council (Exco) line-up for the new term saw six members retained and four new faces appointed to the state administration. The four new Exco members are Md Israk Abdullah (Kukup), P. Pannir Selvam (Perling), Hasrunizah Hassan (Pulai Sebatang) and Dr Muhammad Naqib Md Ghazali (Panti). Md Israk will head the Agriculture, Agro-Based Industry and Rural Development Committee; Pannir Selvam the Unity, Heritage and Culture Committee; Dr Muhammad Naqib the Education and Information Committee; and Hasrunizah the Women, Family and Community Development Committee.

items.title: Police rescue kidnap victim within 48 hours after foiling RM7m ransom plot in Shah Alam
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Police rescued a kidnapping victim within 48 hours of his abduction after foiling an alleged RM7 million ransom plot and arresting six local suspects in a pre-dawn raid on a Shah Alam residence, CID director Datuk M. Kumar said today. He said the suspects are being investigated under Section 3(1) of the Kidnapping Act 1961, which covers kidnapping for ransom. A court has granted police a 10-day remand order for all six suspects under Section 12 of the Kidnapping Act 1961, Kumar said.

items.title: Negri Sembilan polls: Chuah, Lukut and Bagan Pinang contests take shape
items.summary: The Election Commission (EC) confirmed today that Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Datuk Yew Boon Lye will defend Chuah against Pau Jeou Ching of Barisan Nasional (BN) after nominations closed. The first was from the PH candidate at 9.02am, followed by the BN candidate at 9.06am. In Sri Tanjung, incumbent Datuk Dr G Rajasekaran of PH will face BN's A. Achutan, M. Leevineshwaraan of Bersatu, Datuk A. Saravanan, an independent candidate, and Islah Wahyudi Zainudin, also an independent candidate.

items.title: Man, 66, arrested over rape of disabled teen in Tawau
items.summary: TAWAU: A 66-year-old man has been arrested and remanded for seven days over the rape of a 15-year-old girl with disabilities. Acting Tawau police chief Superintendent Wilynton Enchana Watt said the case came to light after the teenager told her adoptive parent that she was experiencing bodily pain. Wilynton said the suspect is being investigated under Section 376(1) of the Penal Code for rape.

items.title: Don’t turn adat into campaign fodder, Mohamad Hasan warns Negeri Sembilan BN party machinery
items.summary: REMBAU, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan has reminded the party’s election machinery not to use Negeri Sembilan’s adat (traditional customs) as a campaign issue in the 16th state election, saying the institution must be protected from political disputes. Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, said the state’s adat institution should be respected and kept separate from politics to prevent unnecessary tensions during the democratic process. “I have made it clear and reminded everyone that during the election campaign, we must not touch on adat issues.

items.title: Lipis route under police watch amid migrant smuggling attempts
items.summary: KUANTAN: The Lipis stretch remains under close police surveillance as smuggling syndicates continue to use the route to ferry undocumented migrants from a neighbouring country into Malaysia. Last year, police arrested 56 undocumented Myanmar nationals – 29 men and 27 women – along with eight Malaysians who acted as transporters for the migrants during a series of integrated operations in Lipis. Pahang acting police chief Datuk Azry Akmar Ayob said the undocumented migrants were believed to have been smuggled through Kelantan before entering Pahang.

items.title: Fahmi says Pakatan will let Tok Min’s track record do the talking as race to keep Negeri Sembilan begins
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 18 — The Pakatan Harapan (PH) campaign for the Negeri Sembilan state election will centre on maintaining administrative continuity and highlighting Menteri Besar Datuk Seri Aminuddin Harun’s track record in leading the state since 2018. PH Communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said the coalition would focus on Aminuddin’s leadership record and the achievements of the state administration, which he said had delivered positive economic outcomes for Negeri Sembilan. Fahmi said he would also monitor the welfare of media practitioners throughout the state election campaign.

items.title: 4 killed, 4 missing in Vietnam flash floods
items.summary: HANOI: At least four people were killed and four others were missing after a flash flood swept through a mountainous village in Vietnam's northern province of Lai Chau, state media reported on Saturday. The flood in Muong Than village early on Friday after days of heavy rains in several parts of northern Vietnam also injured seven people, reported the Vietnam News Agency. Natural disasters, mostly floods, killed 489 people in the country last year, according to government data.

items.title: Nilai turns five-way as DAP’s Arul Kumar faces crowded field
items.summary: SEREMBAN, July 18 — Nilai assemblyman and DAP national vice-chairman J. Arul Kumar faces a tough challenge in a five-cornered contest in the upcoming 16th Negeri Sembilan state election. The Nilai seat has drawn a crowded field, with incumbent Arul Kumar of Pakatan Harapan (PH) up against Zamani Ibrahim (Berjasa), Datuk Lai Chien Kong (Barisan Nasional), Datuk V. Saravana Kumar (Bersatu) and independent candidate Omar Mohd Isa. Over in the Lenggeng seat, PH candidate Zarinna Abu Zarin will take on incumbent Datuk Mohd Asna Amin from BN, alongside Bersatu’s Zool Amali Hussin.

items.title: PH banking on Aminuddin's MB track record in Negri polls, says Fahmi
items.summary: PH communications director Datuk Seri Fahmi Fadzil said Aminuddin's administration had delivered tangible improvements in the economy, development and zakat collection. This will be one of our key campaign messages throughout the state election," he said. Asked about cooperation between Barisan Nasional and Perikatan Nasional, Fahmi said it would not hinder PH's efforts to retain the state.

items.title: A ‘50-50 seat’: Anthony Loke banks on Pakatan’s track record as he fights to retain mixed-majority Chennah
items.summary: JELEBU, July 18 — The Chennah state seat is among the four most crucial constituencies in the Jelebu parliamentary constituency for Pakatan Harapan (PH) as it seeks to retain the Negeri Sembilan government in the upcoming state election, incumbent Anthony Loke said. Earlier, Jelebu Parliamentary returning officer Abdul Rahim A Aziz announced that the Chennah state seat would witness a straight fight between Loke and Barisan Nasional (BN) candidate Siow Kong Choon. Commenting on his chances of retaining Chennah, a mixed-majority seat, following the recent Johor state election in which DAP lost several semi-urban constituencies, Loke said every election has its own dynamics.

items.title: Police warn parties against touching sensitive issues during Negri Sembilan campaign
items.summary: Negri Sembilan police chief Datuk Alzafny Ahmad said candidates and party supporters must refrain from making provocative, inflammatory or defamatory statements that could spark public tension, hatred or racial division. Alzafny also warned that enforcement action would be taken against candidates or supporters who breached traffic and campaign regulations, including riding motorcycles without helmets during election-related activities. Meanwhile, Alzafny said the nomination process for the Negri Sembilan election proceeded smoothly, with all eight nomination centres completing the process without any major issues.

items.title: Onn Hafiz kicks off second term with ‘no honeymoon’ warning for new Johor exco
items.summary: JOHOR BAHRU, July 18 — Johor Menteri Besar Datuk Onn Hafiz Ghazi has reminded the state’s newly appointed executive councillors (Exco) that there will be no “honeymoon” period, urging them instead to get straight to work in fulfilling their responsibilities to the people. Onn Hafiz said the new term would see continuity in the state administration, with six incumbent executive councillors retained and four new faces appointed to strengthen the state government machinery. The six retained executive councillors are Datuk Mohd Jafni Md Shukor, Mohd Hairi Mad Shah, Mohd Fared Mohd Khalid, Ling Tian Soon, Lee Ting Han and Mohamad Fazli Mohamad Salleh.

items.title: Zahid says BN-Perikatan tie-up is ‘understanding’, not pact, as coalition eyes Negeri Sembilan comeback
items.summary: KUALA LUMPUR, July 18 — Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman Datuk Seri Ahmad Zahid Hamidi today insisted his coalition’s electoral cooperation with Perikatan Nasional (PN) in the Negeri Sembilan state election was an “understanding”, not a formal political pact. Speaking after accompanying BN deputy chairman Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan, who is defending the Rantau state seat, at the nomination centre in Rembau, Zahid said the tactical seat-sharing arrangement would help BN regain control of Negeri Sembilan. “God willing, with this tactical approach, Negeri Sembilan will once again be governed by BN.

items.title: QR code checks and tighter controls: Govt moves to tighten cooking oil subsidy scheme
items.summary: PUTRAJAYA, July 18 — The Domestic Trade and Cost of Living Ministry (KPDN) will scrutinise the Public Accounts Committee’s (PAC) recommendations on cooking oil price control and subsidy management, including expediting the Cooking Oil Stabilisation Scheme System (eCOSS) rollout to facilitate targeted subsidies and address subsidy leakages. He said KPDN welcomed the PAC’s recommendations on the management of cooking oil price controls and subsidies under the ministry, as presented in PAC Report DR. 27 of 2026 in the Dewan Rakyat on July 16. “Since the Cooking Oil Stabilisation Scheme (COSS) was introduced, the government has not determined quotas for refineries.

items.title: One seat, four candidates: Jeram Padang stands out in Negeri Sembilan polls as only four-way clash
items.summary: JEMPOL, July 18 — Jeram Padang is the only seat in the Jempol state constituency to witness a four-cornered contest in the 16th Negeri Sembilan State Election, with Serting and Palong seeing three-cornered contests and Bahau set for a straight fight. The Jeram Padang state seat will see Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate G. Manivannan go up against incumbent Datuk Mohd Zaidy Abdul Kadir of Barisan Nasional (BN), R. Sri Sanjeevan of Parti Pribumi Bersatu Malaysia (Bersatu) and Dayana Dal of Parti Orang Asli Malaysia (ASLI), the only Orang Asli candidate vying for the seat. Meanwhile, the Serting state seat is set for a three-cornered battle between PH candidate Yaacob Mahmood, incumbent Mohd Fairuz Mohd Isa of Perikatan Nasional (PN) and Muhammad Noraffendy Mohd Salleh @ Affendy Salleh of Bersatu.

items.title: Rantau showdown set as Umno veteran Tok Mat takes on young doctor from Pakatan
items.summary: REMBAU, July 18 — The Rantau state seat is set for a straight fight in the 16th Negeri Sembilan state election, with state Barisan Nasional (BN) chairman and Foreign Minister Datuk Seri Mohamad Hasan facing Pakatan Harapan (PH) candidate Dr Azizul Hakim Mahdi. Mohamad, who is also Umno deputy president, has held the Rantau state seat since 2004. In the Chembong state seat, incumbent and BN candidate Datuk Zaifulbahri Idris will face a straight fight against PH candidate Danish Nazran Murad.
